label: Field Service Report
description: Technician visit report with photos, equipment notes, and follow-up.

fields.label: Equipment involved
fields.help: Make/model, lane/gate identifier, asset tag if applicable.

fields.label: Parts used
fields.placeholder: Part name | part number | qty — one per line

generation_notes:
Voice: Technical field-report. Factual, chronological, specific. Written as if the technician is documenting for their own records and a supervisor who wasn't there.

Structure:
- Visit Details (date, site, technician, equipment — direct data)
- Issue Description (what was reported, observed state on arrival)
- Work Performed (chronological actions taken, specific components touched, readings taken)
- Parts Used (itemized if any)
- Photos (reference embedded images by caption if images provided)
- Follow-Up (any return visit needed, parts orders placed, escalations)
- Technician Attestation ("I certify the above is accurate. [Technician name], [Date]")

Tone examples:
  CORRECT: "Arrived 09:15. Unit displayed fault code E14 on primary controller. Reset controller per manufacturer procedure; fault cleared. Verified operation through 3 cycle tests. Unit operating normally at departure 10:40."
  WRONG: "I went to the site and found some issues with the system. After troubleshooting, I was able to resolve the problem and the system is now working great!"

Never soften or embellish. If the unit is broken, say "Unit inoperative." If the repair is temporary, say "Temporary repair in place pending parts order."

Length target: 300-700 words.
